originalTitle: Heavy rains and flooding kills at least 23 in Nairobi
title: Big Rains Make Big Puddles in Nairobi!
content:
Hello, Little Explorers!

Hello, hello, my super friends! It's your friendly reporter, Kito, here from our amazing continent, Africa! I have some important news to share with you today from a very big city called Nairobi. Nairobi is in a country called Kenya, right here in Africa. It's a place many people call home.

What Happened with the Rain?

Sometimes, rain is wonderful! It helps our plants grow, it fills our rivers, and it makes everything fresh and clean. But sometimes, rain can be *too* much. Like when you fill your cup with water, and it overflows! That's what happened in Nairobi. Recently, there was a lot, a lot, a lot of rain. It rained so hard and for so long that the ground couldn't soak up all the water anymore. Imagine pouring water on a table; it just spreads out, right? That's what the rain did.

What is Flooding?

When there is too much rain, and the water starts to cover places where it shouldn't be, like roads and even houses, we call this "flooding." It means the water comes up very high, like a giant, moving puddle that keeps getting bigger and bigger. In Nairobi, the floods were very big. The water covered many places. It was hard for people to move around. It's like trying to walk through a very deep swimming pool just to get to your friend's house! But imagine the water is brown and dirty.

Roads Like Rivers!

Do you know what roads are for? They are for cars, buses, and people to walk on. But with all the rain, many roads in Nairobi became like big, brown rivers! The water was so deep that cars could not drive through it. It was too dangerous. When a road is covered in water like that, we say it is "submerged." Imagine your little toy boat floating on your bathtub water. Now imagine the road being the bathtub, and the water is so high that cars can't move. That's what it was like. This made it very hard for mummies and daddies to go to work or for anyone to visit their friends or family. It also made it hard for food to get to the markets.

Did You Know?

Did you know that Nairobi is the capital city of Kenya? A capital city is like the main, most important city in a country. It's usually where the government works!

Aeroplanes Had a Hard Time Too

Nairobi has a very big airport. An airport is where big aeroplanes take off and land. These aeroplanes fly people to different cities and countries. They also carry important things like medicines and food. Because of the very heavy rain and big floods, the aeroplanes found it hard to fly safely. This meant that some aeroplanes could not leave Nairobi, and some could not land there. We say the flights were "disrupted." It's like when your game gets stopped because the rain starts. Imagine you are waiting for your auntie to come visit on an aeroplane, but the aeroplane cannot fly because of the rain. That makes everyone a little sad and worried, doesn't it?

Why is This Important?

Flooding can be very dangerous. The water can be very strong, and it can carry things with it. It's not safe to play in floodwater because it can be dirty and might have hidden dangers. Sadly, because the rains were so heavy and the floods so big, some people got very, very hurt. And sadly, some people did not make it. Our hearts are sad for them and their families. This is why we always need to be careful when there is a lot of rain.

Did You Know?

Did you know that the word "Nairobi" comes from a Maasai phrase, "Enkare Nairobi," which means "cool water place"? It's usually a place with nice water, but sometimes, it can have too much!

Staying Safe is Key!

When there is a lot of rain, here are some things to remember: * **Stay inside:** It's best to stay in your house where it's dry and safe. * **Don't play in floodwater:** Even if it looks like fun, floodwater can be very dangerous. It can hide holes or things that can hurt you. * **Listen to grown-ups:** Your parents, teachers, and other grown-ups know what is safe. Listen to them carefully!

Helpful Hands Are Working

Many grown-ups, like policemen, firefighters, and special helpers, are working hard in Nairobi to help everyone. They are helping people who are stuck, and they are trying to make the roads safe again. People are also helping each other, which is what we do in Africa – we come together! It takes time for all the water to go away and for everything to become normal again. But we know that with brave helpers and everyone staying safe, Nairobi will be strong again.

Did You Know?

Did you know that you should never touch an electric wire if it's in water? Electricity and water are a very dangerous mix! Always tell a grown-up if you see a wire near water.

Let's Hope for Sunshine!

We hope the sun will come out soon in Nairobi and help dry up all the water. Until then, remember to stay safe, listen to your grown-ups, and always be kind to those around you. We send our love and strength to everyone in Nairobi! That's all from Kito, your friendly reporter. See you next time, and remember to always learn something new!
summary: Heavy rains caused big floods in Nairobi, Kenya, making roads like rivers and stopping aeroplanes from flying, and sadly, some people got very hurt, so it's important for everyone to stay safe and listen to grown-ups.
whyMatters: This is important because very heavy rain can be dangerous and cause floods that hurt people, stop cars and aeroplanes, and make it hard for everyone to move around and stay safe, so we need to know how to be careful.
